verdict.note: a managed-agent foreground spex wait starts silently — no point-of-use hint to background it; the freeze warning exists only in help prose

managed-agent shell: one prominent stderr hint at wait start (background this — the exit is your wake-up), then identical behavior (same timeout, same exit 1, same stdout); human shell without session env gets no hint.

backend stopped: wait retries correctly BUT at budget exhaustion stdout is empty and exit=1 — identical exit + stdout to the plain-timeout case (A2), no distinct backend-unreachable token; a supervisor cannot tell transport failure from session timeout

backend stopped: wait retries within budget, then stdout 'backend-unreachable' exit 3 (distinct from plain-timeout exit 1 / gone exit 2); reachable-but-broken backend → 'backend-error' exit 3 immediately; both tokens sit outside the session-status vocabulary
